properties.text: The secp256k1 signature recovery in recover_sender must validate that 0 < r < SECP256K1N; any transaction with r == 0 or r >= SECP256K1N must be rejected before public key recovery.

reachability.notes: An attacker can craft transactions with arbitrary r values and broadcast them via P2P. If r validation is bypassed, a forged or invalid sender could be recovered.

properties.severity_justification: Bypassing r range validation would allow sender spoofing, enabling unauthorized fund transfers and consensus failure across the entire network.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: craft a transaction with r=0 or r=SECP256K1N and demonstrate incorrect sender recovery or crash.

properties.text: The secp256k1 signature recovery in recover_sender must validate that 0 < s <= SECP256K1N / 2 (low-s normalization); any transaction with s > SECP256K1N / 2 must be rejected to prevent signature malleability.

reachability.notes: An attacker can compute the high-s complement of any valid signature (s' = SECP256K1N - s) and rebroadcast. If low-s normalization is not enforced, the same transaction has two valid representations, enabling transaction malleability.

properties.severity_justification: Transaction malleability allows changing the transaction hash without invalidating the signature. This can break transaction tracking, cause double-processing, and disrupt protocols relying on transaction hash uniqueness.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: submit a valid transaction, then compute and submit the high-s complement and demonstrate both are accepted.

properties.text: For legacy transactions, recover_sender must correctly distinguish between pre-EIP-155 (v in {27, 28}) and EIP-155 (v = chain_id * 2 + 35 or chain_id * 2 + 36) signatures, and reject any v value that does not match either pattern.

reachability.notes: An attacker can set arbitrary v values in legacy transactions. Incorrect v parsing could lead to using the wrong signing hash, recovering an incorrect sender, or accepting cross-chain replays.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect v handling could allow cross-chain replay attacks or sender spoofing, both of which can cause fund loss.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: replay a mainnet transaction on a testnet (or vice versa) by exploiting v value ambiguity.

properties.text: For typed transactions (AccessList, FeeMarket, Blob, SetCode), recover_sender must validate that y_parity is either 0 or 1; any other value must cause rejection.

reachability.notes: Attacker can set y_parity to arbitrary values in typed transactions. Invalid parity could lead to undefined secp256k1 recovery behavior.

properties.severity_justification: Invalid y_parity could cause incorrect sender recovery or panics in the cryptographic library, leading to node crashes.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: submit a typed transaction with y_parity > 1 and observe the node behavior.

properties.text: Each transaction type must use its own dedicated signing hash function; the signing hash for a given transaction type must include all fields of that type. Specifically: signing_hash_pre155 covers (nonce, gas_price, gas, to, value, data); signing_hash_155 appends (chain_id, 0, 0); signing_hash_2930 prefixes with 0x01 and includes access_list; signing_hash_1559 prefixes with 0x02 and includes max_priority_fee and max_fee; signing_hash_4844 prefixes with 0x03 and includes max_fee_per_blob_gas and blob_versioned_hashes; signing_hash_7702 prefixes with 0x04 and includes authorizations.

reachability.notes: If a signing hash omits a field, an attacker could modify that field without invalidating the signature, enabling selective tampering.

properties.severity_justification: Missing a field from the signing hash would allow undetectable transaction tampering. An attacker could modify gas, value, data, or access lists after the transaction is signed.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: modify a field not covered by the signing hash and show the signature still verifies.

properties.text: The sender address derived from recover_sender must equal the last 20 bytes of keccak256(recovered_public_key). The derivation must be deterministic: the same (tx, signature) pair must always produce the same sender address.

reachability.notes: Non-deterministic sender derivation would be a consensus failure: different nodes could disagree on which account sent a transaction.

properties.severity_justification: Non-deterministic sender recovery causes consensus splits. Different nodes would execute the same transaction against different sender accounts.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that two nodes derive different senders from the same valid transaction.

properties.text: Pre-EIP-155 transactions (signing_hash_pre155) must NOT include chain_id in the signed fields. This means pre-EIP-155 transactions are inherently replayable across chains.

reachability.notes: An attacker can replay any pre-EIP-155 transaction from one chain to another. This is a known limitation but implementations must correctly handle both pre-155 and post-155 paths.

properties.severity_justification: Known design limitation affecting legacy transactions only. Modern wallets use EIP-155 by default. However, incorrect handling could cause confusion in v-value parsing.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that the implementation either correctly accepts pre-155 txs or incorrectly applies chain_id protection to them.

properties.text: EIP-155 signing_hash_155 must append (chain_id, 0, 0) to the signed fields. A transaction signed with chain_id X must not verify on chain_id Y where X != Y.

reachability.notes: If chain_id is not correctly bound into the signing hash, cross-chain replay attacks become possible for EIP-155 transactions.

properties.severity_justification: Cross-chain replay would allow draining funds from accounts on one chain using transactions signed for another chain.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: replay an EIP-155 transaction from one chain on a different chain_id.

properties.text: Each typed transaction signing hash must be prefixed with the correct type byte: 0x01 for AccessList (EIP-2930), 0x02 for FeeMarket (EIP-1559), 0x03 for Blob (EIP-4844), 0x04 for SetCode (EIP-7702). A type mismatch between the transaction wrapper and the signing hash would enable cross-type signature reuse.

reachability.notes: If type prefix is not included in the signing hash, an attacker could take a signed EIP-1559 transaction and re-wrap it as a different type, potentially changing its semantics.

properties.severity_justification: Cross-type signature reuse could change transaction semantics (e.g., converting a regular call into a contract creation) while maintaining a valid signature.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: take a signed type-2 transaction, re-encode as type-1, and demonstrate it verifies.

properties.text: decode_transaction must be the inverse of encode_transaction for all five transaction types. For any valid transaction T: decode_transaction(encode_transaction(T)) == T.

reachability.notes: Crafted malformed RLP could cause encode/decode asymmetry, potentially leading to consensus divergence if different nodes interpret the same bytes differently.

properties.severity_justification: Encode/decode asymmetry would cause consensus failure: nodes would disagree on the contents of a transaction within a block.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a byte sequence that decodes to different transactions in different implementations.

properties.text: decode_transaction must raise TransactionTypeError for any unknown type prefix byte (i.e., any byte not in {0x01, 0x02, 0x03, 0x04} for typed transactions). Unknown types must not be silently ignored or fallback-decoded.

reachability.notes: An attacker can broadcast transactions with arbitrary type prefix bytes. If unknown types are not rejected, they could be interpreted as a valid type through fallthrough logic.

properties.severity_justification: Accepting unknown transaction types could bypass type-specific validation or lead to node crashes during processing.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: submit a transaction with type prefix 0x05 or 0xFF and demonstrate it is not rejected.

properties.text: validate_transaction must reject any transaction where max(intrinsic_gas, calldata_floor) > tx.gas by raising InsufficientTransactionGasError. The intrinsic gas and calldata floor must be calculated correctly per EIP-7623.

reachability.notes: An attacker can submit transactions with minimal gas but large calldata. If the calldata floor (EIP-7623) is not enforced, calldata-heavy transactions could underpay.

properties.severity_justification: Bypassing gas validation enables DoS through underpaying for computation and calldata. This can bloat blocks with cheap data transactions.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: submit a transaction with gas < calldata_floor but gas >= intrinsic_gas and show it is accepted.

properties.text: validate_transaction must reject any transaction where tx.nonce >= U64.MAX_VALUE by raising NonceOverflowError per EIP-2681.

reachability.notes: An attacker can craft transactions with nonce = U64.MAX_VALUE. If not rejected, nonce overflow could wrap around to 0, enabling replay of all previous transactions.

properties.severity_justification: Nonce overflow wrapping to 0 would re-enable replay of all historical transactions for that account, causing massive fund loss.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate nonce overflow behavior by submitting tx with nonce = U64.MAX_VALUE.

properties.text: validate_transaction must reject contract creation transactions (tx.to == Bytes0) where len(tx.data) > MAX_INIT_CODE_SIZE by raising InitCodeTooLargeError per EIP-3860.

reachability.notes: An attacker can submit contract creation transactions with oversized initcode. If not bounded, this enables memory exhaustion attacks during EVM execution.

properties.severity_justification: Unbounded initcode size could cause memory exhaustion, leading to node crashes (DoS). The initcode is loaded into memory during contract creation.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: submit a contract creation with initcode > MAX_INIT_CODE_SIZE and show it is executed.

properties.text: validate_transaction must reject any transaction where tx.gas > TX_MAX_GAS_LIMIT (16,777,216) by raising TransactionGasLimitExceededError per EIP-7825.

reachability.notes: An attacker can submit transactions with excessive gas limits. Without the EIP-7825 cap, a single transaction could consume an entire block's gas or more, enabling DoS.

properties.severity_justification: Accepting transactions above TX_MAX_GAS_LIMIT could cause excessive EVM execution time, degrading node performance and potentially causing timeouts.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: submit a transaction with gas > 16,777,216 and show it passes validation.

properties.text: calculate_intrinsic_cost must correctly count zero bytes (1 token) and non-zero bytes (4 tokens) in transaction data per EIP-7623. The calldata floor must equal tokens * FLOOR_CALLDATA_COST + TX_BASE_COST.

reachability.notes: An attacker can craft calldata with specific zero/non-zero byte patterns. Incorrect token counting would miscalculate the calldata floor, enabling data-heavy transactions at reduced cost.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect calldata cost calculation would allow block space subsidization via data-heavy transactions, enabling state bloat and economic attacks.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: submit a transaction where calldata tokens are miscounted, resulting in reduced gas cost.

properties.text: calculate_intrinsic_cost must add TX_CREATE_COST (32,000) plus init_code_cost for contract creation transactions (tx.to == Bytes0). Non-creation transactions must not include the create cost.

reachability.notes: If TX_CREATE_COST is not added for contract creation, deploying contracts becomes cheaper than intended, enabling mass contract deployment attacks.

properties.severity_justification: Missing create cost would reduce the economic barrier for contract deployment, but the impact is limited to gas pricing discrepancy.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: submit a contract creation and show intrinsic cost does not include 32,000 create cost.

properties.text: For SetCodeTransaction (EIP-7702), calculate_intrinsic_cost must add PER_EMPTY_ACCOUNT_COST multiplied by the number of authorizations in the authorization list.

reachability.notes: If authorization cost is not charged, an attacker can include unlimited authorizations at zero cost, potentially overwhelming delegation processing.

properties.severity_justification: Free authorizations would enable mass EOA delegation attacks, potentially locking many EOAs to malicious contracts.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: submit a SetCodeTransaction with many authorizations and show the gas cost does not scale.

properties.text: encode_transaction must handle exactly five transaction types: LegacyTransaction (no prefix), AccessListTransaction (0x01), FeeMarketTransaction (0x02), BlobTransaction (0x03), SetCodeTransaction (0x04). Any other type must raise an Exception.

reachability.notes: encode_transaction is called internally after transaction construction. An attacker cannot directly invoke it with an arbitrary type.

properties.severity_justification: Internal correctness property. Failure would indicate a programming error in the client, not an external attack vector.

properties.text: LegacyTransaction encoding must not include a type prefix byte. All typed transactions must include their respective type prefix byte before the RLP-encoded payload.

reachability.notes: Encoding is done internally. However, if legacy transactions erroneously include a prefix, they would be indistinguishable from typed transactions during decoding.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect encoding could cause consensus divergence between implementations that encode/decode differently.

properties.text: get_transaction_hash must produce a unique, deterministic hash for every distinct transaction. For LegacyTransaction, the hash is keccak256(rlp.encode(tx)). For typed transactions, the hash is keccak256(type_prefix + rlp.encode(tx)).

reachability.notes: Transaction hashes are used for transaction identification and receipt matching. Non-deterministic hashing would cause receipt mismatches across nodes.

properties.severity_justification: Non-deterministic transaction hashing would break receipt lookup, transaction tracking, and potentially consensus.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate two nodes producing different hashes for the same transaction.

properties.text: signing_hash_7702 must bind the authorizations field to the signature. This ensures an attacker cannot modify, add, or remove authorization entries after the transaction is signed.

reachability.notes: If authorizations are not bound to the signature, an attacker could add unauthorized delegations to a signed transaction, delegating an EOA to a malicious contract.

properties.severity_justification: Unbinding authorizations from the signature would allow an attacker to insert malicious code delegations, gaining full control over the victim's EOA.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: modify the authorizations field of a signed EIP-7702 transaction and show the signature still verifies.

properties.text: begin_transaction must increase the snapshot count by exactly 1. After begin_transaction, len(state._snapshots) == old(len(state._snapshots)) + 1.

reachability.notes: begin_transaction is invoked for every CALL/CREATE during EVM execution. An attacker can trigger it via nested contract calls. If snapshot count is wrong, rollback would restore to the wrong state.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect snapshot counting would cause rollback_transaction to restore to the wrong state, potentially persisting reverted changes or losing committed ones.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: construct a contract that triggers nested calls and demonstrate snapshot count mismatch.

properties.text: begin_transaction must create deep copies of the main trie, all storage tries, and all transient storage tries. Mutations to the live state after snapshotting must not affect snapshot data.

reachability.notes: If copies are shallow, mutations to live state corrupt the snapshot. On rollback, the snapshot would contain the mutated state rather than the original, making reverts ineffective.

properties.severity_justification: Shallow copies would make rollback_transaction ineffective, allowing reverted contract calls to persist their state changes. This breaks the fundamental atomicity guarantee of the EVM.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: construct a contract that modifies storage in a nested call, revert the call, and show the storage change persists.

properties.text: commit_transaction must decrease the snapshot count by exactly 1. After commit_transaction, len(state._snapshots) == old(len(state._snapshots)) - 1.

reachability.notes: commit_transaction is invoked when a CALL/CREATE succeeds. Incorrect snapshot count after commit could cause later rollbacks to restore the wrong state.

properties.severity_justification: Snapshot count mismatch after commit would cause state management corruption, potentially losing committed state or preserving states that should have been discarded.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: trigger a sequence of begin/commit/begin/rollback and show state inconsistency.

properties.text: commit_transaction must clear created_accounts only when the last (outermost) snapshot is popped (len(state._snapshots) == 0 after pop). Inner commits must not clear created_accounts.

reachability.notes: created_accounts is used by get_storage_original and EIP-6780 SELFDESTRUCT constraints. Premature clearing during inner commits would cause incorrect original storage values and incorrect SELFDESTRUCT behavior.

properties.severity_justification: Premature created_accounts clearing would cause incorrect gas refund calculations (EIP-6780) and incorrect get_storage_original returns, leading to consensus divergence.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: construct nested calls where a contract is created in an inner call, then verify created_accounts status after inner commit.

properties.text: rollback_transaction must restore the main trie and all storage tries to the exact state captured in the most recent snapshot. After rollback, the state must be byte-identical to the state at the time of the corresponding begin_transaction.

reachability.notes: rollback_transaction is invoked when a CALL/CREATE reverts. An attacker's contract can deliberately trigger reverts after making state changes. If rollback does not fully restore state, those changes persist.

properties.severity_justification: Incomplete rollback is the fundamental reentrancy vulnerability: state changes from a reverted call persist, allowing attackers to extract value or corrupt state.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: modify storage and balance in a nested call, revert, and demonstrate the changes persist.

properties.text: rollback_transaction must restore transient storage to the exact state captured in the most recent snapshot. Transient storage changes made after begin_transaction must be fully reverted.

reachability.notes: Transient storage (EIP-1153) follows the same snapshot/rollback lifecycle. If transient storage is not restored on rollback, information can leak between call frames.

properties.severity_justification: Transient storage leakage across reverted calls could enable information disclosure or reentrancy-like attacks using TLOAD/TSTORE.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: set transient storage in a nested call, revert, then read the transient storage from the parent call.

properties.text: get_account must never return None. For any address, it must return either the actual Account object from the trie or EMPTY_ACCOUNT (nonce=0, balance=0, code=b'').

reachability.notes: get_account is called for every account access during EVM execution. Returning None would cause a NoneType error, crashing the node.

properties.severity_justification: If get_account returns None, any subsequent attribute access would cause a runtime panic, crashing the node (DoS).
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: interact with a non-existent account and demonstrate the node crashes.

properties.text: get_account_optional must return None for addresses that do not exist in the main trie. It must not return EMPTY_ACCOUNT for non-existent accounts.

reachability.notes: get_account_optional distinguishes between 'account exists but is empty' and 'account does not exist'. Confusing these two states affects account_exists, is_account_alive, and modify_state behavior.

properties.severity_justification: Confusing non-existence with empty account would cause incorrect account lifecycle behavior, potentially preventing empty account cleanup or incorrectly treating non-existent accounts as existing.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that a non-existent account is treated as existing (or vice versa).

properties.text: set_storage must assert that the account exists in the main trie before allowing storage writes. Writing storage to a non-existent account must cause an assertion failure.

reachability.notes: A contract could attempt to write storage to an address where the account was destroyed in the same transaction. If the assertion is missing, orphaned storage entries would be created.

properties.severity_justification: Orphaned storage entries would bloat the state trie indefinitely and could never be cleaned up, as there is no account to reference them.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: destroy an account and then attempt SSTORE to that address in the same transaction.

properties.text: set_storage must clean up empty storage tries: if after trie_set the trie data is empty (trie._data == {}), the storage trie must be deleted from state._storage_tries.

reachability.notes: An attacker's contract can set all storage values to zero. If empty tries are not cleaned up, the state trie grows without bound.

properties.severity_justification: Missing cleanup causes state bloat (empty tries accumulate). This is a gradual DoS that increases node storage requirements over time.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: set all storage keys to zero and verify the storage trie entry persists.

properties.text: get_storage must return U256(0) for any storage key that has not been explicitly set. The return value must always be of type U256.

reachability.notes: Contracts rely on unset storage returning 0. Returning any other value (None, garbage) would break virtually every smart contract.

properties.severity_justification: Non-zero default for unset storage would break the fundamental assumption of every smart contract, causing massive consensus divergence and potential fund loss.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: read a storage slot that was never written and show it returns non-zero.

properties.text: storage_root and state_root must only be computed when no snapshots are active (assert not state._snapshots). Computing roots during a transaction must cause an assertion failure.

reachability.notes: Root computation is called by the block processing pipeline after all transactions are finalized. An attacker cannot directly trigger root computation during a transaction.

properties.severity_justification: Computing roots with active snapshots could produce incorrect state roots, causing consensus failure. However, this is only triggerable by an implementation bug.

properties.text: storage_root must return EMPTY_TRIE_ROOT for any address that does not have a storage trie in state._storage_tries.

reachability.notes: Incorrect storage root for accounts without storage would cause state root mismatch and consensus failure.

properties.severity_justification: Wrong storage root is a direct consensus failure, but it is only reachable through the internal root computation pipeline.

properties.text: state_root must incorporate all per-account storage roots via the get_storage_root callback. The state root must change if and only if the state or any account's storage has changed.

reachability.notes: If storage roots are not incorporated into the state root, storage changes would not be reflected in block hashes, enabling silent state corruption.

properties.severity_justification: Missing storage root incorporation would cause state roots to not reflect storage changes, enabling undetectable state corruption.

properties.text: is_account_alive must return True only if the account exists in the trie AND is not equal to EMPTY_ACCOUNT. It must return False for non-existent accounts and for accounts that exist but are empty.

reachability.notes: is_account_alive is used by the EVM for operations like EXTCODESIZE, BALANCE, and self-destruct checks. Incorrect liveness determination affects EVM execution semantics.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect account liveness affects multiple EVM opcodes and could cause consensus divergence between implementations.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that an empty account is incorrectly reported as alive (or vice versa).

properties.text: modify_state must automatically destroy accounts that become empty after modification (nonce == 0, code == b'', balance == 0). This is the EIP-161 state clearing rule.

reachability.notes: An attacker's contract can transfer all ether from an account with nonce=0 and no code, making it empty. If auto-destruction fails, empty accounts accumulate, bloating state.

properties.severity_justification: Missing state clearing causes gradual state bloat. While not an immediate attack, it degrades node performance over time.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: create a scenario where an account becomes empty but is not destroyed.

properties.text: move_ether must conserve total ether: the sender's balance decrease must exactly equal the recipient's balance increase. Formally: old(sender.balance) - new(sender.balance) == new(recipient.balance) - old(recipient.balance) == amount.

reachability.notes: move_ether is invoked for every ether transfer (CALL with value, transaction value transfer). If ether conservation is violated, tokens can be created or destroyed.

properties.severity_justification: Ether conservation violation would allow unlimited ether minting or burning, fundamentally breaking the economic model of the chain.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a transfer where sender.balance_decrease != recipient.balance_increase.

properties.text: move_ether must raise AssertionError if sender.balance < amount. The balance check must occur before any state modification (sender balance reduction or recipient balance increase).

reachability.notes: An attacker's contract can attempt to transfer more ether than its balance. If the check is after state modification, the sender's balance could underflow.

properties.severity_justification: Balance underflow would allow unlimited ether extraction. This is the classic integer underflow vulnerability applied to the most critical state variable.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: attempt to transfer more ether than the sender's balance and demonstrate balance underflow.

properties.text: increment_nonce must increase the account's nonce by exactly 1. After increment_nonce(state, address), get_account(state, address).nonce == old(get_account(state, address).nonce) + 1.

reachability.notes: Nonce increment is critical for replay protection. If nonce does not increment by exactly 1, replaying the same transaction becomes possible.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect nonce increment would break replay protection, allowing the same transaction to be executed multiple times.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that after a transaction, the nonce did not increment by 1.

properties.text: destroy_account must remove both the account from the main trie AND its storage trie. After destroy_account(state, address): get_account_optional(state, address) == None AND address not in state._storage_tries.

reachability.notes: destroy_account is triggered by SELFDESTRUCT and empty account cleanup. If storage is not removed, orphaned storage entries persist indefinitely.

properties.severity_justification: Orphaned storage from destroyed accounts causes permanent state bloat. The storage can never be cleaned up as there is no account to reference it.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: destroy an account and show its storage trie still exists.

properties.text: destroy_storage must remove the storage trie for the given address only if one exists. If no storage trie exists, destroy_storage must be a no-op.

reachability.notes: If destroy_storage raises an error when no storage trie exists, destroying accounts without storage would crash the node.

properties.severity_justification: If destroy_storage crashes on accounts without storage, any SELFDESTRUCT of a storage-less account would crash the node.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: SELFDESTRUCT an account with no storage and observe a crash.

properties.text: mark_account_created must add the address to state.created_accounts, and this marker must persist even if the account creation subsequently reverts. The marker must only be cleared when the outermost transaction commits.

reachability.notes: created_accounts is critical for EIP-6780 SELFDESTRUCT and gas refund calculations. If markers are cleared on revert, a create-revert-create sequence could bypass EIP-6780 restrictions.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrectly clearing created_accounts on revert would bypass EIP-6780 SELFDESTRUCT restrictions, potentially enabling unauthorized state clearing.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: create a contract, revert, create again, and demonstrate EIP-6780 check gives incorrect result.

properties.text: get_storage_original must return U256(0) for any address in state.created_accounts, regardless of what the outermost snapshot contains. For addresses not in created_accounts, it must read from state._snapshots[0].

reachability.notes: get_storage_original is used for EIP-2200/EIP-3529 gas refund calculations. Incorrect original values lead to incorrect gas refunds, potentially creating a consensus-relevant difference.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect original storage values cause wrong gas refund calculations, leading to consensus divergence between implementations.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: create a contract, write to storage, then show get_storage_original returns a non-zero value.

properties.text: get_transient_storage must return U256(0) for any key that has not been set via set_transient_storage within the current transaction. Transient storage must be empty at the start of each new transaction.

reachability.notes: If transient storage persists across transactions, contracts relying on it for reentrancy guards would be vulnerable to cross-transaction attacks.

properties.severity_justification: Cross-transaction transient storage persistence would break the EIP-1153 security model, potentially enabling bypasses of reentrancy guards based on TLOAD/TSTORE.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: set transient storage in transaction N, then read it in transaction N+1 and show it is non-zero.

properties.text: set_transient_storage must clean up empty transient storage tries: if after trie_set the trie data is empty, the transient trie must be deleted from transient_storage._tries.

reachability.notes: Empty transient tries waste memory within a transaction. An attacker could create many empty transient tries to consume memory.

properties.severity_justification: Transient storage is cleared per-transaction, so memory leak is bounded to one transaction's lifetime. Impact is limited.

properties.text: account_exists must return True for any account present in the main trie, including accounts equal to EMPTY_ACCOUNT. It must return False only for addresses not in the trie.

reachability.notes: account_exists affects EVM access cost calculations (cold vs warm) and EXTCODESIZE behavior. Incorrect results cause gas miscalculation.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect account existence checks cause gas miscalculation (cold/warm access costs), leading to consensus divergence.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate an account existence check returning wrong value and its impact on gas calculation.

properties.text: account_has_code_or_nonce must return False for EMPTY_ACCOUNT (nonce=0, code=b'') and for non-existent accounts. It must return True only if the account has a non-zero nonce or non-empty code.

reachability.notes: Used to prevent contract creation at addresses that already have code or nonce (collision check). Incorrect results could allow deploying code over existing contracts.

properties.severity_justification: If account_has_code_or_nonce returns False for an account with code, CREATE/CREATE2 could overwrite existing contract code, a critical consensus violation.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate CREATE2 overwriting an existing contract because account_has_code_or_nonce incorrectly returns False.

properties.text: close_state must release all state references: _main_trie, _storage_tries, _snapshots, and created_accounts must all be deleted. After close_state, any access to these fields must raise an error.

reachability.notes: close_state is called during block finalization. An attacker cannot trigger it. Failure to release references is a memory leak, not a security vulnerability.

properties.severity_justification: Memory leak from unreleased references. Would only cause degraded performance over many blocks, not a direct security issue.

properties.text: set_account_balance must set the balance to the exact specified amount (not add or subtract). After set_account_balance(state, address, X): get_account(state, address).balance == X. Empty account cleanup via modify_state must apply.

reachability.notes: set_account_balance is used for coinbase payments and fee distribution. Incorrect balance setting would cause wrong fee distribution.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect balance setting during fee distribution would cause consensus divergence and potentially incorrect validator rewards.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that set_account_balance produces a different balance than specified.

properties.text: set_code must set the account's code to the exact bytes provided. After set_code(state, address, code_bytes): get_account(state, address).code == code_bytes. If code is set to empty bytes with nonce=0 and balance=0, the account must be destroyed via modify_state cleanup.

reachability.notes: set_code is called during contract creation (after initcode execution). If code is not set correctly, the deployed contract would have wrong bytecode.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect code deployment would cause the wrong contract to be deployed, potentially enabling unauthorized access to funds or consensus divergence.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: deploy a contract and show the stored code differs from the initcode return value.

properties.text: account_has_storage must check only for the existence of a storage trie for the address in state._storage_tries, not whether the trie contains entries. An address with an allocated but empty storage trie must return True.

reachability.notes: account_has_storage semantic differences between implementations could cause consensus divergence.

properties.severity_justification: Semantic disagreement on what 'has storage' means could cause subtle consensus issues between implementations.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate divergent behavior based on account_has_storage interpretation.

properties.text: The Merkle Patricia Trie implementation must be correct: trie_get, trie_set, copy_trie, and root operations must faithfully implement the authenticated data structure. The root hash must uniquely commit to the trie contents (collision resistance of keccak256).

reachability.notes: Trie correctness is a foundational assumption. A bug here would be critical but is not directly triggerable by external input patterns.

properties.severity_justification: Trie bugs would cause state corruption, consensus failures, and potentially fund loss across the entire network.

properties.text: set_storage must effectively delete a key when the value is set to U256(0). After set_storage(state, address, key, U256(0)): get_storage(state, address, key) == U256(0) AND the key must not occupy space in the trie.

reachability.notes: If setting to zero does not delete the key, storage slots can never be reclaimed, causing permanent state bloat.

properties.severity_justification: Non-deletable storage entries cause state bloat and affect gas refund calculations (SSTORE zero-to-zero transitions).
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: set storage to zero and verify the key still occupies trie space.

properties.text: The set_account function must correctly handle setting an account to None: it must delete the account from the main trie via trie_set(state._main_trie, address, None). This deletion must not affect the account's storage trie.

reachability.notes: set_account is called internally by modify_state and destroy_account. If setting to None also deleted storage, accounts could lose storage during temporary account deletion/recreation.

properties.severity_justification: If set_account(None) also deleted storage, account recreation in the same transaction would lose storage, causing consensus divergence.

properties.text: The EVM must correctly enforce gas metering after validate_transaction confirms sufficient gas. After intrinsic gas deduction (including the EIP-7623 calldata floor and EIP-7825 TX_MAX_GAS_LIMIT), execution must not exceed the remaining gas budget.

reachability.notes: If gas metering is bypassed, an attacker could craft a transaction that executes indefinitely, hanging the node.

properties.severity_justification: Gas metering bypass would enable infinite execution loops, causing nodes to hang indefinitely (complete DoS).
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a transaction that executes more gas worth of computation than it paid for.

properties.text: Under EIP-7702 (SetCodeTransaction), authorization delegations must be signed by the delegating EOA. The authorization must be bound to the transaction signature via signing_hash_7702. However, once delegated, the contract code has unrestricted access to the EOA's state -- this is an inherent trust assumption, not a bug.

reachability.notes: An attacker could attempt to forge authorization tuples. If authorization validation is weak, unauthorized code delegation could occur.

properties.severity_justification: Forged authorizations would allow an attacker to delegate an EOA's execution to a malicious contract, gaining full control over the EOA's assets.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: forge an authorization tuple and demonstrate it is accepted without the EOA's signature.

properties.text: Deeply nested transactions (many begin_transaction calls) must be bounded by gas costs for CALL/CREATE operations. The TX_MAX_GAS_LIMIT (EIP-7825) must prevent unbounded nesting depth that could exhaust memory via snapshot deep copies.

reachability.notes: An attacker can create a contract that calls itself recursively to maximum depth. Each level creates a deep copy. If gas doesn't adequately bound this, memory exhaustion (OOM) can crash the node.

properties.severity_justification: Memory exhaustion via deep nesting could crash nodes (OOM). The gas cost per nesting level must be sufficient to prevent this within TX_MAX_GAS_LIMIT.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: construct a contract with maximum recursive call depth and measure memory consumption.

properties.text: A reentering contract must not be able to exploit the gap between balance checks and state updates in move_ether. The checks-effects-interactions pattern in the sequential state machine and the rollback mechanism must prevent extracting more ether than authorized.

reachability.notes: Classic reentrancy attack vector. The attacker deploys a contract with a fallback function that re-enters move_ether. If balance is checked before the previous transfer completes, the same balance can be spent multiple times.

properties.severity_justification: Reentrancy in move_ether would allow draining all ether from a contract in a single transaction.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: deploy a reentering contract that exploits move_ether to extract more ether than its balance.

properties.text: An attacker must not be able to craft a transaction that, when decoded, is interpreted as a different transaction type than intended. The strict type checking in decode_transaction (Legacy via RLP object type, typed via unique prefix bytes 0x01-0x04) must prevent type confusion.

reachability.notes: An attacker can craft bytes that could be ambiguous between a Legacy RLP object and a typed transaction. The distinction between Bytes (typed) and non-Bytes (legacy) must be unambiguous.

properties.severity_justification: Type confusion would bypass type-specific validation rules, potentially allowing a SetCodeTransaction to be interpreted as a regular transfer.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: craft a byte sequence that could be decoded as two different transaction types.

properties.text: The get_storage_original function must read from the outermost snapshot (state._snapshots[0]) only. If snapshot boundaries are incorrect, values from different transaction contexts could be returned, leaking information.

reachability.notes: A contract can call get_storage_original indirectly via SSTORE gas refund calculations. If it reads from the wrong snapshot index, gas refunds would be incorrect.

properties.severity_justification: Reading from the wrong snapshot would produce incorrect gas refunds, leading to consensus divergence. In extreme cases, it could leak pre-transaction values from unrelated accounts.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: create a nested call scenario where get_storage_original returns the inner snapshot value instead of the outermost.

properties.text: An attacker must not be able to flood the transaction processor with invalid transactions to consume excessive validation resources. validate_transaction must enforce early rejection via gas checks, nonce overflow, initcode size, and gas limit before expensive operations.

reachability.notes: An attacker can broadcast many invalid transactions from the P2P network. Signature validation (secp256k1 recovery) is the most expensive validation step. If performed before cheap checks, DoS amplification is possible.

properties.severity_justification: If expensive validation (signature recovery) occurs before cheap checks (gas limit, nonce), an attacker can exhaust CPU resources with minimal cost.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that submitting transactions with gas > TX_MAX_GAS_LIMIT triggers expensive signature verification before the gas limit check.

properties.text: The RLP encoding must be deterministic and injective: encode_transaction and decode_transaction must be inverse operations. Distinct transactions must always produce distinct encodings. This ensures the signing hash uniquely identifies a transaction.

reachability.notes: If RLP encoding is non-injective (two different transactions produce the same encoding), signing one transaction would authorize the other.

properties.severity_justification: Non-injective encoding would allow signature reuse across semantically different transactions, enabling unauthorized state mutations.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: find two distinct transactions that produce the same RLP encoding.

properties.text: The keccak256 hash function used for signing hashes, state roots, storage roots, and address derivation must be collision-resistant. The implementation must match the standard keccak-256 specification.

reachability.notes: A keccak256 implementation bug would affect all hash-dependent operations: signatures, state roots, address derivation, and transaction hashing.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect keccak256 implementation would cause consensus failure, signature verification errors, and incorrect address derivation across the entire network.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that the implementation produces a different hash than the reference keccak-256 for the same input.

properties.text: The signing_hash_4844 must bind max_fee_per_blob_gas and blob_versioned_hashes to the signature. Modifying either field after signing must invalidate the signature.

reachability.notes: If blob parameters are not bound, an attacker could modify the blob_versioned_hashes, potentially substituting malicious blobs or changing the blob gas fee.

properties.severity_justification: Unbound blob parameters would allow blob content substitution, breaking the data availability guarantee for rollups.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: modify blob_versioned_hashes in a signed EIP-4844 transaction and show the signature still verifies.

properties.text: modify_state must apply the modification function to the current account state before checking for empty account cleanup. The modification must use the frozen dataclass modify pattern, not direct mutation.

reachability.notes: Direct mutation of frozen dataclass would bypass the trie update mechanism, leaving the trie inconsistent with the in-memory account object.

properties.severity_justification: Bypassing the modify pattern could cause trie inconsistency, where the in-memory account differs from the trie-stored account.

properties.text: Transient storage must follow the same trie data structure as persistent storage (Trie with secured=True and default=U256(0)). Both must be created with identical parameters to ensure consistent behavior.

reachability.notes: If transient storage uses different trie parameters (e.g., unsecured or different default), behavior would diverge from persistent storage, potentially leaking key preimages.

properties.severity_justification: Inconsistent trie parameters between persistent and transient storage could cause subtle behavioral differences exploitable in edge cases.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate behavioral difference between transient and persistent storage due to trie parameter mismatch.

properties.text: The signing_hash_2930 must bind the access_list to the signature. An attacker must not be able to modify, add, or remove access list entries after the transaction is signed.

reachability.notes: If the access list is not bound to the signature, an attacker could add entries to pre-warm storage slots, changing gas costs and potentially affecting contract execution.

properties.severity_justification: Unbound access lists would allow manipulation of gas costs for specific storage accesses, potentially enabling gas-based attacks on contracts with gas-sensitive logic.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: modify the access_list of a signed EIP-2930 transaction and show the signature still verifies.

properties.text: The signing_hash_1559 must bind both max_priority_fee_per_gas and max_fee_per_gas to the signature. An attacker must not be able to modify fee parameters after the transaction is signed.

reachability.notes: If fee parameters are not bound, an attacker could increase the max_fee to drain the sender's account or decrease it to underpay for inclusion.

properties.severity_justification: Unbound fee parameters would allow draining sender accounts by inflating max_fee, or disrupting the fee market by deflating fees.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: modify max_fee_per_gas of a signed EIP-1559 transaction and show the signature still verifies.

coverage.trust_boundaries_not_covered_justificati: tb-007 (P2P to validator transaction ordering/censorship) and tb-008 (validator to transaction processor) are out-of-scope for this specification layer; they concern validator behavior which is not enforceable at the execution layer.
coverage.subgraph_invariants_covered: All 44 invariants from 26 state subgraphs and all invariants from 12 transaction subgraphs are represented
